ground_truth.title: Today's Trending Articles on Digital Marketing and Media | EMARKETER
ground_truth.main_content: EMARKETER's trending articles covering digital marketing, media, retail, technology, and financial services. Featured article: 'Our favorite episodes of 'Reimagining Retail'' - four podcast episodes to queue up for holiday travel. The retail brands that thrived in 2025, and those that struggled - retailers with a well-defined identity delivered strong growth. Agentic AI browsers face a towering barrier to entry - Google Chrome controls 73.2% of worldwide internet traffic, more than five times the combined share of all non-Safari competitors. Checking in: Reviewing our 2025 retail predictions - retailers would offer richer in-store experiences to attract shoppers, with updates on Amazon, discount retailers, and dollar stores. Truist's direct deposit switching tool is a play for instant loyalty - creating a direct-deposit relationship is a well-worn path to primary FI status. Monzo leans into Gen Z homebuying journey with mortgage broker acquisition - UK neobank acquires Habito as it expands homeownership features. TikTok's US overhaul gives advertisers greater certainty, though questions remain - Oracle, Silver Lake, and MGX will hold 50% ownership with US-appointed directors overseeing data protection. 5 of the most controversial campaigns of 2025. Brand invisibility is the new threat as 62% of B2B CMOs fall behind AI-native rivals - AI discovery is rewriting the rules of B2B marketing. YouTube's AI creator avatars could be a future ad play—if consumers accept them - experimenting with AI avatars based on a small group of popular creators.
with: Our favorite episodes of 'Reimagining Retail' - Here are four 'Reimagining Retail' episodes to queue up for your holiday travel.
The retail brands that thrived in 2025, and those that struggled - Retailers with a well-defined identity delivered strong growth in 2025.
Agentic AI browsers face a towering barrier to entry - Google Chrome controls 73.2% of worldwide internet traffic, more than five times the combined share of all non-Safari competitors.
TikTok's US overhaul gives advertisers greater certainty, though questions remain - TikTok has agreed to a sweeping US restructuring that creates a majority-American-controlled joint venture.

Brand invisibility is the new threat as 62% of B2B CMOs fall behind AI-native rivals - Nearly two-thirds (62%) of B2B tech marketing leaders lack the skills, budget, or strategy to compete with AI-native firms.
without: Browse articles featuring EMARKETER's latest data and insights on digital marketing
